mistakes.description: Session acquired a second issue lock while still holding a lock on a different issue, causing a deadlock where the worktree isolation hook blocked all file edits
mistakes.root_cause: SYSTEM: work-prepare had no check for an existing session lock before acquiring a new one. A session holding lock A could invoke /cat:work again and acquire lock B, but the worktree isolation hook only registers the first worktree (A), blocking all edits in the second worktree (B). The resulting deadlock left the agent unable to work on either issue.
mistakes.correct_behavior: Before acquiring a new issue lock, work-prepare checks whether the calling session already holds a lock on a different issue. If it does, return ERROR immediately with guidance to complete or release the existing issue first.

mistakes.description: Agent merged three issues without presenting the Step 8 Approval Gate to the user
mistakes.root_cause: DUAL CAUSE: (1) AUTO-FIX LOOP PRIMING: work-with-issue/first-use.md stated "The user already approved the workflow by invoking /cat:work" with no qualification, overgeneralizing to suppress all user interaction including the mandatory Step 8 approval gate. (2) HOOK BYPASS GAP: M480 enforcement hook only covered the Task tool; agents could bypass it via the Skill tool (issue 1) or direct Bash invocation of merge-and-cleanup (issue 2). Three bypass routes existed but only one was blocked.
mistakes.correct_behavior: After completing the auto-fix loop, execute Steps 6, 7, and 8 in sequence. Step 8 (Approval Gate) MUST present AskUserQuestion to user when trust != "high". Hook enforcement now covers Task tool, Skill tool, and direct Bash invocation of merge-and-cleanup.

mistakes.description: Agent invoked cat:work-merge via the Skill tool instead of the Task tool, causing the merge phase to be skipped entirely while the agent proceeded to call work-complete and report success
mistakes.root_cause: The work-with-issue skill instructs Step 9 to spawn a merge subagent using the Task tool with subagent_type: cat:work-merge. The main agent used the Skill tool instead, which only loads skill content and returns 'Launching skill: cat:work-merge' without executing any merge operations. The agent mistook the Skill tool's success: true response for a completed merge and proceeded to work-complete without verifying the branch moved.
mistakes.correct_behavior: Use Task tool (subagent_type: cat:work-merge) to spawn the merge subagent. After the Task tool returns, verify the merge happened by checking that TARGET_BRANCH now contains the squashed commit before invoking work-complete.

mistakes.description: Agent manually squashed commits using git -C <path> reset --soft instead of delegating to cat:git-squash (Step 7), bypassing the hook that was intended to block this pattern
mistakes.root_cause: HOOK REGEX GAP: RemindGitSquash.java used pattern 'git\s+reset\s+--soft' which only matches when 'reset' immediately follows 'git'. The command 'git -C <path> reset --soft' has '-C <path>' between 'git' and 'reset', so the pattern did not match and the hook allowed the prohibited operation through. No test existed for this variant, so the gap was never detected.
mistakes.correct_behavior: Use /cat:git-squash (Step 7) to squash commits. The RemindGitSquash hook now matches 'reset --soft' anywhere in the command, blocking both 'git reset --soft' and 'git -C <path> reset --soft' forms.

mistakes.description: Agent squashed 9 implementation commits into 2 separate commits (feature: + test:) instead of one, because the git-squash skill's default topic grouping contradicted commit-types.md which defines feature:/test:/refactor:/docs: as ONE implementation commit category
mistakes.root_cause: DOCUMENTATION CONTRADICTION: The git-squash skill Critical Rules listed 'feature: vs docs: -> keep separate' but commit-types.md defines all implementation types (feature:, bugfix:, test:, refactor:, docs:) as ONE implementation squash category. The agent correctly followed the skill topic-grouping guidance, which conflicted with commit-types.md doctrine that test commits covering issue implementation belong in the same commit as the implementation code.
mistakes.correct_behavior: All implementation-type commits (feature:, bugfix:, test:, refactor:, docs:) for the same issue belong in ONE implementation commit. Test commits that verify the issue implementation are part of the implementation, not separate topics. Only split implementation from config/infrastructure (config: for general dependencies/tooling).

mistakes.description: Main agent triggered cat:learn for CONCURRENT_MODIFICATION warnings after git-squash returned OK, because the skill documentation did not explain that these warnings are informational and expected when the base branch has advanced since branch creation.
mistakes.root_cause: MISSING DOCUMENTATION: The git-squash SKILL.md Result Handling section documented status codes (OK, REBASE_CONFLICT, VERIFY_FAILED, ERROR) but did not document the CONCURRENT_MODIFICATION warning type. Agents receiving an OK response with warnings had no guidance on whether this was expected, what it meant, or whether to escalate to cat:learn. The absence of documentation caused the agent to treat normal informational warnings as a mistake requiring learn.
mistakes.correct_behavior: CONCURRENT_MODIFICATION warnings in an OK git-squash response are informational. They indicate files were modified on both branches and rebase auto-resolved them. Verify working tree is clean, verify auto-resolution is correct for each flagged file, then proceed normally. Do NOT trigger cat:learn for correctly auto-resolved concurrent modifications.

mistakes.description: Subagent investigated wrong failure event - focused on isModelInvocable robustness instead of agent's skill execution model misconception
mistakes.root_cause: Missing documentation explaining that Skill tool execution is synchronous (loads content into context in same turn). Agent incorrectly assumed Skill tool spawns async subprocess and waited for background completion that never occurred.
mistakes.correct_behavior: Skill tool loads content synchronously into agent context in same conversation turn. After receiving skill content, agent immediately executes skill steps within conversation flow - no waiting for background processes.

mistakes.description: Agent edited client/ source files directly in the main workspace (/workspace) instead of the issue worktree, because the user said 'direct fix' and the EnforceWorktreePathIsolation hook only blocks edits when the current session holds a lock — it did not block edits from a session that had no lock but an active worktree existed
mistakes.root_cause: HOOK GAP: EnforceWorktreePathIsolation.check() returned allow() when WorktreeContext.forSession() returned null (no lock for the current session). The hook was designed for the normal case where the editing session holds a lock, but did not handle the case where a separate session starts a 'direct fix' in the same project while a worktree is active. The lock file contained the session ID of the session that created the worktree, not the current session's ID, so the check found no context and allowed the edit.
mistakes.correct_behavior: All plugin/ and client/ code changes — including user-requested 'direct fix' changes — must be made in an issue worktree. The EnforceWorktreePathIsolation hook now falls back to scanning all active worktrees when no session lock exists, blocking edits to paths that any active worktree covers.

mistakes.description: Agent added (M453) provenance labels to Java Javadoc and inline comments, violating the no-retrospective-commentary rule
mistakes.root_cause: DOCUMENTATION PRIMING: CLAUDE.md uses (Mxxx) parenthetical labels (e.g., (M352), (M267), (M430), (M453)) as provenance markers for its own rules. The agent read CLAUDE.md and inferred this notation was acceptable to use in Java source code comments, applying it to EnforceWorktreePathIsolation.java. The no-retrospective-commentary rule in common.md covered this case but CLAUDE.md did not disclaim that its (Mxxx) labels are scope-limited to CLAUDE.md itself, leaving agents to infer the pattern was general-purpose.
mistakes.correct_behavior: Java source code comments and Javadoc must describe current behavior only. (Mxxx) provenance labels belong exclusively in CLAUDE.md rule annotations, not in source code. When reading (Mxxx) labels in CLAUDE.md, do not infer they are a general-purpose notation for Java files.

mistakes.description: Agent did not invoke cat:learn-agent after load-skill failed with 'catAgentId is required' error; DetectFailures hook warning used advisory language ('Consider') instead of mandatory language, allowing the agent to skip learn invocation
mistakes.root_cause: The DetectFailures PostToolUse hook correctly detected the failure (exit code non-zero, output matched FAILURE_PATTERN containing 'Exception') and emitted a warning. However, the warning used advisory language: 'Consider: 1. Fix the immediate issue 2. If this could recur, use learn skill to implement prevention'. The agent interpreted this as optional and continued without invoking learn. The hook was designed to suggest rather than mandate, so no enforcement mechanism existed to require learn invocation after tool failures.
mistakes.correct_behavior: When DetectFailures detects a bash command failure matching the FAILURE_PATTERN, the hook warning must use mandatory language: 'MANDATORY: Invoke cat:learn-agent to record this mistake and implement prevention. Do NOT continue to the next step without invoking learn first.' This removes ambiguity and requires the agent to invoke learn before proceeding.

mistakes.description: Agent continued mid-operation squash/rebase workflow without using TaskCreate for two user messages
mistakes.root_cause: RECURRENCE OF M337: The InjectSessionInstructions documentation says 'ADD the user's request to TaskList' and marks it MANDATORY, but the agent deferred both user messages until after the entire squash/rebase workflow completed without creating TaskCreate entries. The agent treated the workflow boundary (merge complete) as the trigger to address user messages, not the next tool call boundary as documented. Documentation-level prevention cannot override completion bias when the agent judges that the workflow is nearly done.
mistakes.correct_behavior: When a user message arrives mid-operation (detected via queue-operation enqueue in JSONL), call TaskCreate immediately in the next tool call for any request that is a feature, change, fix, or complex instruction — before continuing the workflow. A PostToolUse hook should detect unacknowledged user input in session JSONL and inject a reminder.

mistakes.description: Agent invoked cat:work-merge via Skill tool instead of Task tool, then described merge as executing when nothing was running (recurrence of M451)
mistakes.root_cause: RECURRENCE OF M451: Agent used Skill tool (loads instructions only) instead of Task tool (spawns subagent) for cat:work-merge. The work-with-issue skill clearly documented 'NOT the Skill tool' and M451 added synchronous-execution docs to skill-loading.md, but both documentation-level preventions failed. The agent violated the protocol by using Skill tool and then described the merge as executing and waited forever.
mistakes.correct_behavior: Use Task tool with subagent_type: cat:work-merge to spawn a merge subagent. The Skill tool only loads instructions into context — it does NOT spawn a subprocess or execute merge operations. EnforceApprovalBeforeMerge should BLOCK Skill tool invocations of cat:work-merge with a clear error directing use of Task tool instead.

issue_creation_needed.suggested_title: Block Skill tool invocation of cat:work-merge via EnforceApprovalBeforeMerge
issue_creation_needed.suggested_description: EnforceApprovalBeforeMerge currently allows Skill tool invocations of cat:work-merge after approval. It should instead BLOCK Skill tool invocations entirely (allowing only Task tool). This prevents the M451/M457 recurrence where agents use the Skill tool and describe the merge as executing when nothing runs.
suggested_acceptance_criteria: EnforceApprovalBeforeMerge blocks Skill tool invocations of cat:work-merge with message explaining to use Task tool
Task tool invocations of cat:work-merge still pass through the approval check normally
Tests verify Skill tool is blocked and Task tool is allowed after approval
work-with-issue first-use.md uses BLOCKING framing for the Task-tool-only requirement

mistakes.description: Merge subagent resolved REBASE_CONFLICT manually (violating Step 4 STOP rule) and left orphaned backup branch after successful retry
mistakes.root_cause: The work-merge skill Step 1 REBASE_CONFLICT handler specifies no cleanup of the backup_branch returned in the JSON response. When git-squash returns REBASE_CONFLICT, it creates a backup branch for safety; the skill only says to return CONFLICT status and STOP, but gives no instruction to delete the backup branch. When an agent ignores the STOP instruction and resolves manually, the backup branch is never cleaned up — but even if STOP is followed, the backup branch remains orphaned.
mistakes.correct_behavior: When git-squash returns REBASE_CONFLICT, immediately delete the backup_branch from the response JSON before returning CONFLICT status. The backup branch is a pre-squash safety snapshot that serves no purpose once squash is abandoned or retried successfully.

mistakes.description: Agent proceeded to merge after AskUserQuestion returned empty answers object, assuming approval without verified user selection
mistakes.root_cause: Missing guidance in work-with-issue/first-use.md Step 8 for the case where AskUserQuestion toolUseResult.answers is an empty object {}. The skill said "wait for explicit user selection" but did not specify how to detect or handle an empty/unrecorded answer. The agent saw the success message "User has answered your questions: ." (with nothing after the colon), recognized uncertainty, but defaulted to approval assumption instead of STOP.
mistakes.correct_behavior: After invoking AskUserQuestion, inspect toolUseResult.answers. If answers is empty {}, treat as no consent and re-present the approval gate. Only proceed when answers contains a non-empty selection.

mistakes.description: work-complete/first-use.md INVOKE passed $0 (session_id) instead of $1 (completedIssue) and $2 (targetBranch) to get-output-agent, causing GetIssueCompleteOutput to receive 2 session_id args and throw 'Expected 1 or 2 arguments, got 2'
mistakes.root_cause: The work-complete/first-use.md INVOKE line used wrong positional variable references: 'args="work-complete $0 $1"' where $0=session_id and $1=session_id (both resolve to catAgentId). The correct references are $1=completedIssue and $2=targetBranch per the argument-hint '<completedIssue> <targetBranch>' in SKILL.md. The documentation taught the wrong arg indices, causing the agent to pass catAgentId twice as handler args.
mistakes.correct_behavior: In first-use.md, $0 is the catAgentId (session_id), $1 is the first positional arg (completedIssue), and $2 is the second positional arg (targetBranch). INVOKE must use 'args="work-complete $1 $2"' to pass the issue name and target branch as handler args.

mistakes.description: AutoLearnMistakes Pattern 11 false-positive: 'critical issues' in stakeholder-review severity table matched 'CRITICAL ISSUE' regex (case-insensitive), triggering spurious learn-agent invocations during normal Bash cat of skill documentation
mistakes.root_cause: Pattern 11 in AutoLearnMistakes.java used CASE_INSENSITIVE matching for 'CRITICAL (DISASTER|MISTAKE|ERROR|FAILURE|BUG|PROBLEM|ISSUE)' without requiring first-person/self-referential context. The stakeholder-review SKILL.md severity table contains 'Must fix critical issues' and calibration rules using 'critical issues', 'critical error' as domain vocabulary. When agents cat this file, the output passes through filterGitNoise (which does not filter skill documentation lines) and the severity-table content matches the self-acknowledgment pattern, causing spurious mistake detection.
mistakes.correct_behavior: Pattern 11 must require first-person/self-referential framing before triggering critical_self_acknowledgment. Match phrases like 'I made a CRITICAL error' or 'this was a CRITICAL MISTAKE', not bare severity-table vocabulary like 'critical issues' or 'Must fix critical issues'.

issue_creation_needed.suggested_title: Fix AutoLearnMistakes Pattern 11 false-positive on severity vocabulary
issue_creation_needed.suggested_description: AutoLearnMistakes.java Pattern 11 uses case-insensitive regex 'CRITICAL (DISASTER|MISTAKE|ERROR|FAILURE|BUG|PROBLEM|ISSUE)' which matches severity-table vocabulary like 'critical issues' in stakeholder-review/SKILL.md.
suggested_acceptance_criteria: Pattern 11 does NOT trigger on 'Must fix critical issues' or 'critical error' in severity tables
Pattern 11 DOES trigger on 'I made a critical error' or 'I caused a critical mistake'
Pattern 11 DOES trigger on 'this was a catastrophic mistake'
Tests in AutoLearnMistakesTest verify all false-positive and true-positive cases
No regression in other AutoLearnMistakes patterns

mistakes.description: Agent merged issue 2.1-add-backup-branch-cleanup-to-merge without verifying AskUserQuestion answers were non-empty, and skipped mandatory skill-builder review for modified skill file plugin/skills/work-merge-agent/first-use.md
mistakes.root_cause: MISSING GUIDANCE: work-with-issue/first-use.md Step 8 described 'wait for explicit user selection' and 'Unknown consent = No consent = STOP' but did not specify HOW to detect selection — the agent must inspect toolUseResult.answers and treat empty {} as no-consent. The structural gap (no inspection protocol) allowed the agent to proceed when answers={} by assuming approval from the success message 'User has answered your questions: .' Additionally, the skill-builder pre-gate check was documented but the agent skipped it for a modified skill file, violating the Step 8 pre-gate sub-step.
mistakes.correct_behavior: After AskUserQuestion returns, inspect toolUseResult.answers. If answers is empty {}, treat as no consent and re-present the approval gate. Only proceed when answers contains a non-empty selection matching one of the presented options. Before presenting the approval gate, always check whether any skill/command files were modified and invoke cat:skill-builder for each one — this step is MANDATORY and BLOCKING.

issue_creation_info.suggested_title: Add answers-inspection protocol to work-with-issue Step 8 approval gate
issue_creation_info.suggested_description: work-with-issue/first-use.md Step 8 must explicitly instruct the agent to inspect toolUseResult.answers after AskUserQuestion. If answers is an empty object {}, the agent must treat this as no consent and re-present the gate. The current text says 'Unknown consent = No consent = STOP' but does not specify how to detect unknown consent from the toolUseResult JSON. This is a recurrence of M459 (prevention_implemented: false) and M446.
suggested_acceptance_criteria: Step 8 specifies that toolUseResult.answers must be inspected after AskUserQuestion
Step 8 specifies that empty answers {} = no consent = re-present the gate
Step 8 specifies the correct path: only proceed when answers contains a matching option label
Empirical test confirms agent inspects answers and re-presents gate when empty
Empirical test confirms agent does NOT proceed with empty answers

mistakes.description: Haiku subagent assumed user approval when AskUserQuestion returned empty answer string, proceeding with merge without explicit user consent
mistakes.root_cause: MISSING GUIDANCE: work-with-issue/first-use.md Step 8 instructs agent to wait for explicit user selection and states 'Unknown consent = No consent = STOP', but does not define how to detect an empty/ambiguous AskUserQuestion response. When the tool returned 'User has answered your questions: .' with no selection text, the haiku subagent applied completion bias and assumed approval. Third recurrence of the same structural gap (M446 → M459 → M462 → M463).
mistakes.correct_behavior: After AskUserQuestion returns, inspect toolUseResult.answers. If answers is an empty object {}, the visible signal is "User has answered your questions: ." with nothing after the colon. Treat this as no consent and re-present the approval gate. Only proceed when answers contains a non-empty selection matching one of the presented options.

issue_creation_info.suggested_description: Issue fix-approval-gate-empty-answers-handling already exists and is open. This is the 3rd recurrence (M446 → M459 → M462 → M463). The fix requires adding explicit answers-inspection protocol to work-with-issue/first-use.md Step 8.
suggested_acceptance_criteria: Step 8 specifies that AskUserQuestion response must contain a non-empty selection
Step 8 specifies that blank/empty answer = no consent = re-present the gate
Empirical test confirms agent re-presents gate when answer is empty
Empirical test confirms agent does NOT proceed with empty answer

mistakes.description: Approval gate for issue 2.1-fix-approval-gate-empty-answers-handling proceeded despite AskUserQuestion returning empty answers {} three consecutive times — same structural gap that M463 was meant to fix
mistakes.root_cause: MISSING GUIDANCE (4th recurrence): work-with-issue-agent/first-use.md Step 8 did not specify that empty toolUseResult.answers means no consent. The fix for this gap was being implemented in the very issue whose approval gate triggered the same failure. The skill file in the worktree branch contained the fix, but the running subagent was using the cached/loaded version without the fix, so the empty-answers guidance was not in its active context.
mistakes.correct_behavior: After AskUserQuestion returns, inspect toolUseResult.answers. If answers is empty {}, the visible signal is 'User has answered your questions: .' with nothing after the colon. Re-present the approval gate. Only proceed when answers contains a non-empty selection matching one of the presented options.

mistakes.description: Test mistake description
mistakes.root_cause: Test root cause
mistakes.correct_behavior: Test correct behavior

mistakes.description: AutoLearnMistakes false-positives on build_failure and test_failure patterns triggered by session-analyzer JSON output whose 'text' field values contain embedded JSON strings with those keywords
mistakes.root_cause: FILTER GAP (recurrence of M461 root cause): filterJsonContent() only strips lines that start with '{' or '[{' and contain specific JSON markers. session-analyzer output lines like '    "text" : "{\"...build_failure...\"}'" start with whitespace and a quote, not '{', so they pass through the filter. The embedded keyword content inside the 'text' JSON field then matches Patterns 1 (build_failure) and 2 (test_failure), producing false positives even when no real build or test failure occurred.
mistakes.correct_behavior: filterJsonContent() must also strip lines that match the pattern of JSON string value lines (e.g., lines starting with whitespace + '"text"' or any quoted key) so that embedded content from session-analyzer JSON output does not trigger false-positive mistake detection.

issue_creation_info.suggested_title: Fix AutoLearnMistakes false-positives on session-analyzer output
issue_creation_info.suggested_description: AutoLearnMistakes.filterJsonContent() does not filter 'text' field lines from session-analyzer JSON output. These lines look like '    "text" : "{\"...build_failure...\"}'" — they start with whitespace and pass through the filter, causing Patterns 1 and 2 to match embedded content and fire false-positive mistake hooks. The fix should extend filterJsonContent() to also strip lines whose content is a JSON string value that could contain embedded keywords. This is a recurrence of M461 (same filter gap, different trigger source).
suggested_acceptance_criteria: filterJsonContent() strips 'text' field lines from session-analyzer JSON output
Pattern 1 (build_failure) does NOT trigger when session-analyzer output contains 'build_failure' in a 'text' field
Pattern 2 (test_failure) does NOT trigger when session-analyzer output contains 'test_failure' in a 'text' field
Real build failures (BUILD FAILURE in direct maven output) still trigger Pattern 1
Real test failures (Tests run: X, Failures: N with N>0) still trigger Pattern 2
Tests in AutoLearnMistakesTest verify all false-positive and true-positive cases for session-analyzer output
No regression in other AutoLearnMistakes patterns

mistakes.root_cause: The sed pattern uses a 9-character hash when searching the git rebase todo file, but git rebase -i uses 7-character abbreviated hashes. The mismatch caused the pattern to silently fail.
Orchestrator delegation prompt described the GOAL but not the MECHANISM. Subagent chose weaker mechanism. Verify subagents also accepted the weaker interpretation.
Skill preprocessor does not perform environment variable substitution on backtick-quoted expressions in SKILL.md files.
Agent defaulted to asking user permission for a potentially impactful action (auto-fix loop) despite the workflow explicitly pre-approving this step.

mistakes.description: When /cat:retrospective was invoked and skill-loader returned pre-rendered analysis output, the agent echoed the output verbatim but did NOT execute workflow steps 5-9 (identify new patterns, derive action items, create escalations, write retrospective record, reset counter). The skill first-use.md explicitly requires: when output starts with Analysis data, output verbatim THEN continue with Steps 5-9. The agent missed the post-handler requirement and left the mistake counter unreset (38 mistakes) with no patterns or action items recorded in index.json.
mistakes.root_cause: Incomplete instruction parsing - agent focused on immediate action (echo) without reading the full workflow continuation that follows.
mistakes.correct_behavior: When /cat:retrospective outputs Analysis data, the agent must execute post-handler Steps 5-9 without being prompted: identify new patterns, derive action items, create escalations, write retrospective record to split file, reset counter, and present results to user.

mistakes.root_cause: The audit implementation subagent misinterpreted the scope of 'search and update all STATE.md examples' as including creation of new issue directories. The subagent created planning files for issues that don't exist on the target branch, likely from generating examples or confusing issue exploration with issue creation.

The learn subagent (haiku model) did not follow the phase-investigate.md instructions to search the JSONL for evidence before analyzing root cause. It fabricated a plausible-sounding but incorrect root cause without examining the git commands that actually created/staged the files. The pre-extracted investigation context provided only keyword matches and did not include the git add -A command at line 339.
Existing rule not enforced as a hard constraint.

The stakeholder-review skill or its invocation does not respect the patience matrix decision logic. When a concern's benefit is less than cost*2 (with configured trust level), the concern should be silently deferred without user escalation. The agent incorrectly invoked AskUserQuestion after the patience matrix had already decided the concern should be deferred.
git-rebase skill Result Handling table omits backup deletion for OK status, priming agents to trust backup_cleaned JSON field instead of executing explicit deletion
